title: Cirrus F30
body: Test and incorporate new F30 image and a fix for a very ugly google vs cloud-init race post-build.

@edsantiago while I'm finalizing this one,  IIRC there was some small typo or somesuch you wanted me to fix?
It was a trivial one: `contrib/cirrus/setup_environment.sh`, 'resuze' -> 'resize2fs'
@edsantiago thanks...got it.
@mheon @baude @giuseppe @edsantiago PTAL F30 is ready to go
LGTM
/hold

*sigh*

My old-friend, the "failure-to-expand disk" problem is back, now on F30 (previously was F29) :confused:
/me jumps up and down cursing: I thought I fixed the disk-space problem :sob:

Seems I did fix _one_ disk-resize problem, but there is still some kind of race between google services and cloud-init.  I suspect it's over networking availability.  Attempting to duct-tape in an alternate cloud-init unit file.
:cry: nope, still didn't get it, cloud-init seems more broken :confounded:
Okay, I think I got it finally...
...confirmed, probably fixed (based on 4/4 positive builds)
/hold cancel
disk space problem is fixed, but testing on F30 is failing (seems legit breakage).

```
vfs driver does not support mount options
```
Yeah, are people actually aware of this? It caused me a lot of grief with my skopeo tests. Basically, it means "you have to comment out the mountopt line in /etc/containers/storage.conf"
@edsantiago You mean `mountopt = "nodev"`?
@rhatdan It sounds like the default storage.conf is incompatible with VFS?

>You mean mountopt = "nodev"?

Yes. Any attempt to use `vfs` fails unless you also delete or comment out that line.

@edsantiago thanks for jumping in on the failure cause.

Rebased this against #3239 to help confirm that works in F30 (here)

We could make podman smart enough to handle this, or force the user to do 
Looks like this might be a bug, if you are just doing --storage-driver=vfs
According to the man pages.
```
man podman
...
       --storage-driver=value

       Storage driver.  The default storage driver for UID 0 is configured in /etc/containers/storage.conf
       ($HOME/.config/containers/storage.conf in rootless mode), and is vfs for non-root users when
       fuse-overlayfs is not available.  The STORAGE_DRIVER environment variable overrides the default.  The
       --storage-driver specified driver overrides all.

       Overriding this option will cause the storage-opt settings in /etc/containers/storage.conf to be
       ignored.  The user must specify additional options via the --storage-opt flag.
```
According to the last two lines, using a storage-driver should set the storage-opt to ""

> According to the last two lines, using a storage-driver should set the storage-opt to ""

Ahh okay there is a ``--storage-opt`` that's what Matt and I couldn't find.  In that case, @rhatdan fix in #3240 is the correct behavior.   I rebased this PR against Dan's fix PR, let's see if that gets us green CI...
Rebased against #3272 early, assuming it merges will make final testing here go faster.
/approve

This LGTM, but only on Monday. Let's not make any CI changes on a Friday, heh.

> This LGTM, but only on Monday. Let's not make any CI changes on a Friday, heh.

:+1: 

'cept I won't be here next week.  

Also, at this late-stage, this PR may need to be manually merged as there's a bit of a catch-22:  

* We have current issues that affect F30 (blocking merge of this PR)
* Not having F30 in CI is allowing new issues to merge in undetected

I'll monitor this in case a re-run can fix a test flake, otherwise the above will only get worse with time :confounded:
/lgtm
